meaning: n.面容,脸色,支持vt.支持,赞助
examples: Jake would not countenance Janis's marrying while still a student.
The committee refused to countenance his proposals.
Gradually his countenance cleared, and he began to smile long, slow smiles.
They'll countenance some very limited tax raising powers in peripheral areas, in return for borrowing rights.
Still, some on the federal bench refuse to countenance such tactics in their more brazen variations.
